> There is no limit to the number of lines that may be joined together in this way. However, a backslash must not be used before an [empty line](#empty_line_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> *Example:* The following is legal.
> *Example:* The following is not legal.
> In the examples above, where a line of [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") follows immediately after a line ending in backslash, the backslash acts as a continuation for two lines of [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") and can therefore be used to split 
> More importantly, however, any [information fields](#information_field_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") and [stylesheet directives](#stylesheet_directive_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") are processed (and [comments](#comment_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") are removed) at the point where the ph
> *Example:* The following should be typeset as a single staff in the printed score.
